description:
Problem: the fz2 quicksort trace now proves source re-lexing is gone, but the terminal run still has a separate native backend tail after the compiler2 drive closes. On 2026-06-10, target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry /tmp/fz2_quicksort_12_9.jsonl run fixtures/quicksort/input.fz produced: drive span 57.955 ms, then fz.codegen.compile 47.749 ms from ~58.487 ms to ~106.240 ms before process_exited at ~106.466 ms. This is visible in codegen telemetry, but it is not represented as compiler2 fact work, so drive-only telemetry makes the backend tail look like a gap.

Goal: make native backend time accounted for in the compiler2 artifact boundary without shims/adapters. Either the native backend becomes first-class work/facts with telemetry that composes with the compiler2 drive, or the artifact boundary explicitly reports the post-drive compile/link span so perf traces cannot misattribute it.

Signal: quicksort fz2 trace can explain total compile time from source submission through process exit, with drive time and native backend time both named. No source fragment re-lexing should return.

References: fz-rh2.12.9 measurement; /tmp/fz2_quicksort_12_9.jsonl during that ticket run.

comments.text:
Post-fix measurement from target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry /tmp/fz2_quicksort_12_13.jsonl run fixtures/quicksort/input.fz on 2026-06-10:

- Lexer span-starts remain exactly 4: fixtures/quicksort/input.fz, runtime:runtime.fz, runtime:Kernel.fz, runtime:Process.fz.
- fz.compiler2.drive: 58.109 ms.
- fz.compiler2.native_backend.compile: 47.207 ms, metadata backend=jit, root_id=0, backend_revision=1, entry_fn_id=0, body_count=42, callable_entry_count=0.
- Nested fz.codegen.compile: 47.136 ms, parent_span_id equals the compiler2 native_backend compile span.
- fz.runtime.process_exited at ~106.034 ms.

The backend tail is now named at the compiler2 artifact boundary; codegen-internal phase accounting remains under fz.codegen.compile.

title: Account post-drive native backend tail in compiler2 telemetry
